enabled_var.help_text: Retrieve the enabled state from the given dict of host variables. The enabled variable may be specified as "foo.bar", in which case the lookup will traverse into nested dicts, equivalent to: from_dict.get("foo", {}).get("bar", default)

enabled_value.help_text: Only used when enabled_var is set. Value when the host is considered enabled. For example if enabled_var="status.power_state"and enabled_value="powered_on" with host variables:{   "status": {     "power_state": "powered_on",     "created": "2020-08-04T18:13:04+00:00",     "healthy": true    },    "name": "foobar",    "ip_address": "192.168.2.1"}The host would be marked enabled. If power_state where any value other than powered_on then the host would be disabled when imported. If the key is not found then the host will be enabled

host_filter.help_text: This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Regex where only matching hosts will be imported.

dtstart.help_text: The first occurrence of the schedule occurs on or after this time.

dtend.help_text: The last occurrence of the schedule occurs before this time, aftewards the schedule expires.

next_run.help_text: The next time that the scheduled action will run.

timezone.help_text: The timezone this schedule runs in. This field is extracted from the RRULE. If the timezone in the RRULE is a link to another timezone, the link will be reflected in this field.

until.help_text: The date this schedule will end. This field is computed from the RRULE. If the schedule does not end an empty string will be returned

related.help_text: Data structure with URLs of related resources.

summary_fields.help_text: Data structure with name/description for related resources.  The output for some objects may be limited for performance reasons.

operation.help_text: The action taken with respect to the given object(s).

changes.help_text: A summary of the new and changed values when an object is created, updated, or deleted

object1.help_text: For create, update, and delete events this is the object type that was affected. For associate and disassociate events this is the object type associated or disassociated with object2.

object2.help_text: Unpopulated for create, update, and delete events. For associate and disassociate events this is the object type that object1 is being associated with.

object_association.help_text: When present, shows the field name of the role or relationship that changed.

action_node.help_text: The cluster node the activity took place on.

object_type.help_text: When present, shows the model on which the role or relationship was defined.
